found a workaround:
Make project using Godot 4.5 win32.exe (select Forward+ or Mobile) then drag project.godot onto the win64.exe
(for some reason, you can switch to Compatibility mode from here, save the project, then drag project.godot and it will open the editor in compatibility mode)

$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --path test_project/ --rendering-method forward_plus -e
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --path test_project/ --rendering-method mobile -e

This one still crashes:
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --path test/ --rendering-method gl_compatibility -e

another workaround:
//these launch and you can make a project (with win64.exe) targeting compatibility, save it, then drag it onto win64.exe and it will open the editor in Compatibility
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --rendering-method forward_plus
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --rendering-method mobile

//crashes with same segfault
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe --rendering-method gl_compatibility

Launch Godot 4.4, 4.4.1, 4.5 or 4.6 (win64.exe)
$ ./Godot_v4.5-stable_win64.exe -d

summary: Copying AnimationLibrary resources(make unique), but no deep duplicate it's sub animation resources.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5stable, 4.4.1stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7628) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12490F (12 threads) - 31.81 GiB memory

### Issue description

When I try to copy AnimationLibrary resources with using make unique, modifying animation resources still affects the original copy. After a long period of trying😅, I found that the correct behavior can only be obtained by enabling make unique for the sub animation resources in the AnimationEditor. This should indicate that when make unique is enabled for animation library resources by default, but not recursive to sub animation resources. I think is a issue because when we want to copy(duplicate_deep) AnimationLibrary resources(make unique), the objects we want to modify are sub animation resources, so we should default to enabling make unique recursion or add another option 'make unique(recursive)'. Maybe related to #100673.

Demo:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/39603012-4396-445f-9c5f-65b4ebd24bec

### Steps to reproduce

1. Copy a AnimationLibrary resource from one to another AnimationPlayer
2. Make it unique
3. Tweak a animation e.g named 'idle' in AnimationLibrary
4. You will find that the original copy be modified at the same time
5. Open 'manage animations' option in the AnimationEditor, make 'idle' sub animation resource unique and then tweak it
6. Check the original copy not modified

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[anim_2025-10-02_02-56-15.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22646751/anim_2025-10-02_02-56-15.zip)

summary: Desktop will hang when clossing window when you press play in editor and then hang the system

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5 Linux

To clarify i use AMD drivers using ROCm 6.4.4 because i need it for blender with linux-6.14. 

To throw a little light on the matter, i used old versions of the engine with the proprietary drivers (that means i had linux-6.11 and ROCm 6.4.2).

In my laptop while following the procedure it works normally when i used 4.4.1. I am guessing some resource are not being release to the system due to but communication with the proprietary drivers? (using Ubuntu's 24.04 default Mesa Drivers).

By default i use the Mesa version drivers that come with Ubuntu 24.04. I once had i similar problem, although it didn't crash the system or crashed the engine, where if i installed the latest Mesa drivers and opened a sub-window the engine will start to get slow and almost unresponsive.

AMD as i can see installs Mesa drivers if i choose open-source version (with ROCm).

This problem happens with both X11 and Wayland Display Drivers.

My project is a 2D mobile game with a mod called admod for ads in android and ios phones. For now i am using in the android manifest a sample id that the developers have in there documentation in order to be able to run and test on mobile.

After project migration i have cleared cache files and settings that the engine needed to re-generate just to be sure, unless i am missing something.

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS 24.04 on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (RADV NAVI21) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 31.23 GiB memory

### Issue description

When i try to run the project in-editor it all works great. When i close the sub-window to stop the play or pause it the engine hangs and then makes my system hang. I have to restart the PC.

Problems happens in 4.5 engine version. The project was imported from 4.4.1 version.

Then i tried opening a new project with nothing in. In forward+ the hang does not happen(lucky?) then when i go to mobile mode the problem occurs.

I tried the same thing on my project with Forward+ and it still hang. The only thing left for me to guess is that a setting that is set in mobile causes problem because i changed a lot of settings in the default fields but let the mobile exclusive features to their default.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Godot Engine 4.5.
2. Create Default template.
3. Click play and agree to default settings in order to make the play window Appear.
4. Try closing the window either by clicking the X window button or pressing stop in the editor.
5. Godot will Hang and then spectacularly crash the system.
6. restart system.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Non sure what kind of file you may need. The problem occurs even when i have a new project.

summary: Set WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED not working on Linux

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5.stable.official.876b29033

### System information

Distributor ID: Ubuntu Description:    Ubuntu 24.04 LTS Release:        24.04

### Issue description

I wrote a test coverage for handling window modes, but it fails on Linux distribution.
When setting the window mode to `WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED` the window is minimized but the `DisplayServer.window_get_mode()` returns `WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED`
And when switch back to `WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED` the window is still minimized.

### Steps to reproduce


Attach the script to a scene and run it by
`godot -S window_mode.tscn`
```gdscript
extends Node2D


func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	set_process(false)
	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED)
	DisplayServer.window_move_to_foreground()
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout

	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED)
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout

	DisplayServer.window_move_to_foreground()
	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED)
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout


	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED)
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout

	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN)
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout

	debug(DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED)
	await get_tree().create_timer(3).timeout
	get_tree().quit()


func debug(display_mode: DisplayServer.WindowMode) -> void:
	DisplayServer.window_set_mode(display_mode)
	var message := "{mode} curent mode: {current_mode} should be {expected_mode}".format({
		"mode" : get_mode_as_string(display_mode),
		"current_mode" : DisplayServer.window_get_mode(),
		"expected_mode" :display_mode
	})

	prints(message)


func get_mode_as_string(display_mode: DisplayServer.WindowMode) -> String:
	match display_mode:
		DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED: return "WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED"
		DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED: return "WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED"
		DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED: return "WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED"
		DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN: return "WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN"
		DisplayServer.WINDOW_MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN: return "WINDOW_MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN"
		_: return ""
```
## Results
- Windows 11
```
WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED curent mode: 0 should be 0
WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED curent mode: 1 should be 1
WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED curent mode: 0 should be 0
WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED curent mode: 2 should be 2
WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN curent mode: 3 should be 3
WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED curent mode: 1 should be 1
```
- Linux
```
WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED curent mode: 0 should be 0
WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED curent mode: 0 should be 1
WINDOW_MODE_WINDOWED curent mode: 0 should be 0
WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED curent mode: 2 should be 2
WINDOW_MODE_FULLSCREEN curent mode: 3 should be 3
WINDOW_MODE_MINIMIZED curent mode: 0 should be 1
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

n/a

summary: Add a new editor theme

description.text: Is with great pleasure that I present you, Godot's soon-to-be new default theme! This is a 1:1 port of @passivestar's [Minimal Theme](https://github.com/passivestar/godot-minimal-theme?tab=readme-ov-file), with himself also helping us behind the curtains in order to facilitate the porting work. The editor font used is now [Inter](https://rsms.me/inter/), and the new theme also changes the editor's base color and accent. While the blue tint had become a staple of Godot’s look over the past years, it caused issues such as distorting the perceived white balance of the viewport. Switching to a neutral gray resolves this. But for those who still liked the older look better, fret not, As **the old theme and color preset is still available**, for you to pick! They can be accessed by choosing "Classic" in the `interface/theme/style` and `interface/theme/preset` settings. While this PR is in a merge-able state, some small things still need to be worked on: - Nested `TabContainer`s blend with each other. - The disabled tab style is a placeholder, while waiting for passive to implement it. - `PopupMenu`s still look very squarish, as #106324 still needs to be resolved. Feedback is more than welcome, but please, keep it civil. Topics involving theming and design are very subjective, so the more objective information you can provide about your issues the easier it will be to address them in future PRs.

**Fixes #93755 , Fixes #111005**

**Current Behavior**

When any object for example ui dock tab or  a invalid file type is near the tree control system , then the tree starts to auto scroll (File System Dock or Scene Manager Dock) , even if the dock cannot accept the drop .

**New Behavior**

if the Tree is a valid target for the currently dragged object . This fix the auto scroll movement of the tree control systems.

**Testing the fix**
1.Open a new project.
2. Move the filesystem dock above the scene manager dock .
3. Make sure the filesystem has multiple files.
4. Try to hover any file or a ui dock name (Scene or Import or Filesystem )  near or over the Scene Manager or vice versa check.

summary: BackBufferCopy rect-mode not working, stops `hint_screen_texture` from being updated

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - Arch Linux 20250413.0.335299 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT (RADV NAVI10) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.25 GiB memory

### Issue description

if the BackBufferCopy is in Rect mode, `hint_screen_texture` does not get updated with the current rendered frame or any piece of it within the bounds of the Rect.
On a freshly opened project, the buffer texture is pure black. BackBufferCopy must be set to Disabled or Viewport to update it.

This behavior is found to be consistent between the forward, mobile, and compatibility renderers.

Could be related to #108867 or #84987, but for me it manifests a little differently. I can't tell how hardware/driver-specific this issue actually is.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8102508a-53b1-4d72-be59-35baccb9ebad

### Steps to reproduce

1: Set up a Node2D scene with background graphics of choice
2: add a BackBufferCopy node and a Sprite2D with any texture
3: add a shader to above mentioned Sprite2D with a shader that uses `hint_screen_texture`. [This one from the docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/shaders/screen-reading_shaders.html) for example.
4: change the copy mode of the BackBufferCopy mode to and from Rect while panning and zooming the editor view and moving the different nodes to see how the buffer in the shader fails to be updated.

[BackBufferCopyTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22627914/BackBufferCopyTest.zip)

summary: Godot project migration from 4.4 to 4.5 crashes Godot on Mac when project folder contains exported Windows .exe file

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

MacOS 14.7.1 (M-series CPU)

### Issue description

I created my project initially on Godot 4.4 on Windows. However, when importing and opening the project in Godot 4.5 on Mac and choosing to migrate the project to 4.5, Godot promptly crashes. When zipping the project folder for this issue, I realized it still contained the .exe files from Windows. Upon deleting those and reopening the project in Godot 4.5 and migrating the project, it succeeds.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a project in the latest version of Godot 4.4 on Windows
2. Create an export of the project (.exe file) in the folder
3. Copy the project folder onto a Mac
4. Import the project into Godot 4.5
5. Choose to migrate the project when opening
6. See that Godot crashes
7. Delete the .exe files, reopen Godot, and migrate again
8. See that it succeeds and does not crash

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

If I include an exported .exe file per the issue, it is more than 10MB here, but if you export the included zipped project and then follow the above steps, you will see the issue.

summary: Allow extracting translation strings without generating POT

description.text:
The POT generation feature has the advantage of automatically extracting translatable strings from various sources (scenes, scripts, and other resources via custom `EditorTranslationParserPlugin`s).

However, not all users like `gettext` workflow; some prefer the out-of-the-box supported CSV translation feature or even implement their own formats (the `Translation` class has a corresponding API for customization). The inability to access standard parsers makes this inconvenient and forces the use of POT generation.

This PR exposes access to standard parsers. Since the `EditorTranslationParser` singleton isn't exposed, access to its methods is provided through static `EditorTranslationParserPlugin` methods. An alternative would be to do this through `EditorPlugin` (as with the `add_parser()` and `remove_parser()` methods), but I think it would be a bad idea to clutter the rather generic `EditorPlugin` with unrelated methods. Also, `EditorTranslationParserPlugin` seems like a pretty expected place for these methods.

Additionally, this PR provides access to the list of built-in strings, which corresponds to the "Add Built-in Strings to POT" checkbox in the editor interface.

This PR slightly breaks compatibility: now custom plugins added later have priority, not earlier ones. On the other hand, this could be considered a bug fix.

summary: Add inspector multi-select editing

description.text:
Allows editing multiple properties in the inspector at the same time. Multiselecting is done with Shift+click, single select with Ctrl+click.

If you select multiple resources, you can edit the same property(s) on multiple resources at a time. Video example:


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0711e0f0-ebde-4b52-a016-55937ea3ae22



This should alleviate Stylebox and theming tedium described in proposals like https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13034, https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/8094, https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/9743.

summary: Fix Quick Open history

description.text:
I don't know if it was like that from the beginning or if it broke at some point, but the EditorQuickOpenDialog's handling of item history was completely wrong.

The idea was that when you open the dialog, it setups the default entries by first appending the recently used ones and then all files. However there were 2 major problems there :D
- The "all files" did not account for the items added from history and were overwriting them
- If a history item was not overwritten, it was possible to have it appear twice (because nothing was checking if a file was already added)

I recently noticed that the quick open history wasn't working properly, and as I investigated, I discovered these 2 funny bugs. This PR fixes them.

summary: Project Manager: Duplicate Project allows duplicate to be placed inside original project

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] 

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6900 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21025.15011) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.91 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using the project manager to create a duplicate of a project, If the user accidentally places the duplicate project inside the folder of the original project the project will try to recursively copy itself. If the project has any large files this will quickly fill up the disk the operation was performed on. Additionally the duplication will never, and can never finish. 

I will fully admit this is user error, however it is an easy mistake to make while migrating projects.

A sanity check to ensure the user isn't trying to create the duplicate within the project they are duplicating would prevent this behavior. Godot already prevents you from creating a duplicate project if the parent directory of the path doesn't exist, so this behavior would be in line with that. Alternatively, if the duplicate project function ignored folders with the name of the duplicated project, this would prevent the recursive loop.

### Steps to reproduce

I suggest performing this reproduction on an empty flash drive as it makes cleanup easier.
Create a project.
Use the project manager duplicate project function to create a duplicate of the project. Set the Project Path in the duplicate project window to a path within the original projects directory.
Press duplicate.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[recursion-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22614321/recursion-test.zip)
You could just use the new project dialog to create this, there is nothing unique about the project file.

summary: Can't run current scene with one key press or click when XR headset is connected

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in `4.5.stable`

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

4.5 introduced replaces the `Run Scene` button and the `Run Specific Scene` button with popup menus that allow the user to select between running in regular mode and running in XR mode.

While having this choice is nice, it does mean that it is now impossible to press a button or a key to run the scene. An additional action is now required. Sometimes, two additional actions are required, because the last-used option is sometimes highlighted, but sometimes not. This means the user cannot even adjust to the new UX by remembering to press two keys in a row, because sometimes it needs more. This also means I can no longer put on my headset and hit a key using muscle memory to hop in. I'm now forced to start the game *before* putting on my headset (which is problematic because sometimes I need to test something that happens right when the game starts).

The keyboard shortcuts are now functionally equivalent to _opening a dropdown_. This is not how shortcuts are typically used in the Godot editor - they are supposed to *perform an operation* or *open a feature*.

### Steps to reproduce

- Have an XR headset connected.
- Press F6 (or whatever the Run Scene shortcut is mapped to).
- Note that the dropdown opens, but you need to make an additional selection to start the game.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

### Issue description

I'm attempting to retrieve image data from an image calculated in a compute shader.  After trying to use my computed Texture2DRD in several Godot components, I noticed that it was causing a lot of weird graphical glitches, so I was thinking if I could just turn it into a regular Image instead, I could at least use that as a stable from of the image.  Since Texture2DRD extends Texture2D, I had assumed that the get_image() method on it would work.  However, when I call it, it causes a crash.  

I looked through the Godot source and the crash seems to be coming from `RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::texture_get_copyable_layout`.  The `tex_info->vk_create_info.extent` struct appears to be uninitialized, which is causing a divide by zero.  Not sure if there is a work around for this.

summary: Change StyleBox content margins type from float to integer.

description.text:
### Tested versions

current master.

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

`StyleBox` content margins are defined in pixels but are handled as floating-point values internally. In editor, however, margins are treated as integers (from the editor inspector), which creates inconsistency.

This leads to problems for `Control` nodes that draw text or rely on `StyleBox::get_margin(Side)` and `StyleBox::get_minimum_size()` for layout:

Example:
- If you call `StyleBox.set_content_margin_all(1.5)`, the left and right margins are both stored as 1.5.
- When calculating the minimum size, `StyleBox::get_minimum_size()` will return `Vector2(3, 3)` (summing the fractional margins).
- During drawing, however, we have to decide whether to ceil, round, or floor these fractional margins to avoid artifacts.

This creates a mismatch:
- For layout, the minimum size is reported with the fractional values summed.
- For drawing, integer rounding is always necessary, otherwise text and controls won’t align correctly on the pixel grid.

As a result, developers will be forced to work around this by manually recalculating the effective minimum size with integer casts and to avoid using `StyleBox::get_minimum_size()`:

```c++
Size2 ms = Size2(
    (int)style->get_margin(SIDE_LEFT) + (int)style->get_margin(SIDE_RIGHT),
    (int)style->get_margin(SIDE_TOP) + (int)style->get_margin(SIDE_BOTTOM)
);
```

Note: I have changed them internally without breaking compatibility and it still fixes the issue, https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110791.

### Steps to reproduce

I have only tested it for `TextEdit` and i have seen other issues related to inconsistent node height that are also caused by floating-point margins, and we may find more related issues for the nodes that draws text and uses `StyleBox::get_minimum_size`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b2b3b46-36fb-43f4-920d-18249ce453b8

summary: Delete "Activate now?" button

description.text: Give one reason why it should stay. |Before|After| |-|-| ||| Typical plugin creation workflow: you open the Plugins tab, select Create New Plugin, type name and optionally other stuff, confirm. The addon is created, you edit the script and do something like ```GDScript extends EditorPlugin var dock: Control func _enter_tree(): dock = load("dock_scene.tscn") add_dock(dock) func _exit_tree(): remove_dock(dock) ``` Then go to Plugins tab again, disable your newly created addon and then enable again so the dock is added. But now you have an error in your Output: `Error: p_dock is null, in remove_dock()`. That's because your plugin was activated while it had no code and tried to deinitialize when you disabled it. However `dock` only exists when you enable the plugin _and_ it actually creates the dock. There is zero reason to activate the plugin immediately. `add_control_to_dock()`, `add_export_plugin()`, `add_context_menu_plugin()` etc. all typically run in `_enter_tree()`, but when your script is just created, this code does not exist yet, so to register your new docks/plugins you have to run `_enter_tree()` again, which means disabling and enabling the script. If you have to disable it to do anything then why even enable it beforehand? It would be maybe fine if it was harmless, but if you deactivate a plugin that wasn't initialized, you end up with useless errors.

summary: Fix editor resources importing

description.text: Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111008 ### Scenario - The current editor scale is 100%. - A new resource is created, flagged as **scale with editor scale** and reimported. - File `.godot/imported/[resource].editor.meta` is created. - The user change editor scale to 200% and restarts editor. - The imported editor resource how still original scale, because reimport was not applied. - Timestamp of resource (or its `.import` file) is still the same, so `_is_test_for_reimport_needed` returns `false`. Note: Same goes for **convert colors with editor theme**. ### Proposal - A new file `.godot/imported/.editor.meta` is introduced. - This file contains editor scale of the last import of editor resources. - The file is loaded during `EditorFileSystem::_scan_filesystem` call and used to determine if editor requests different scale. - This allows to keep timestamp optimization and flag only editor resources for testing for reimport if needed.

summary: Export Template Download Fails in Godot 4.5 (Engine and Website) – Fails Near 1 GB

description.text:
### Tested versions

* Reproducible in: **Godot 4.5.stable** (official build)
* Not tested in earlier versions



### System information

 **OS Edition:** Windows 10 Pro **OS Version:** 22H2 **OS Build:** 19045.6332  **Network:** Wi-Fi

### Issue description

When attempting to download the export templates for Godot 4.5 via the editor (`Project > Install Export Templates`) or manually from the Godot website, the download fails after progressing to around **1 GB**.

The failure message shown is:

```
Godot_v4.5-stable_export_templates.tpz
Failed - Unknown server error. Please try again, or contact the server administrator.
```

**Expected behavior:**
The export templates should fully download and install successfully.

**Actual behavior:**
The download proceeds partway (~1 GB) and then fails with the above error. This suggests a potential timeout, file size limit, or server-side issue.

This issue affects both:

* In-editor template download
* Manual `.tpz` download from the official website


### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Godot 4.5.stable.
2. Go to **Project > Install Export Templates**.
3. Begin download.
4. Observe that it downloads for a while (up to ~1 GB), then fails.
5. Alternatively, attempt manual download of `Godot_v4.5-stable_export_templates.tpz` from the website.
6. Observe the same failure near 1 GB.

summary: Implement the Ground Truth Ambient Occlusion (GTAO) effect

description.text: Resolves , but for 4.x. Showcase: In medium quality, the visual quality of GTAO is comparable to ASSAO, while in Ultra quality, GTAO outperforms ASSAO conspicuously. Note: Due to algorithm differences, the parameters cannot perfectly map between ASSAO and GTAO (for example, ASSAO **intensity 2.0** and GTAO **intensity 1.0** gives visually similar AO strengths). This could be balanced by adding a multiplier for some parameters if required. Togglable by UI: ## Implementation details Main gather logic was taken from the GTAO implementation of Unreal Engine and [flower renderer](https://github.com/qiutang98/flower/blob/main/source/shader/gtao/gtao_evaluate.glsl). Also referenced [XeGTAO](https://github.com/GameTechDev/XeGTAO/blob/master/Source/Rendering/Shaders/XeGTAO.hlsli) to check the correctness of some computational details. Blur and interleave part has reused the edge-aware smart blur that ASSAO also depends on. GTAO isn't much more costly than ASSAO, but as a high-quality effect, it isn't intended for super low-end scenarios. Thus, the effect is only implemented for RendererRD without support for the Compatibility backend. ## Questions Upon implementing the effect, I noticed that the ASSAO gather is run four times with four different depth textures that's packed into a texture array, and then averaged between the four values produced. I don't understand what's the real purpose for this and whether I can remove this logic and only run the gather once for GTAO. Could anyone explain this for me? ## Possible future improvements A "full" implementation of GTAO would have to include a temporal filter pass. The current implementation omits the temporal filtering step, as lacking it won't considerably make the visual quality degenerate (if a better result is needed, enabling TAA would compensate for the lack of temporal filtering, though it still looks fine without TAA enabled). Temporal filtering could be added in the future through a separate PR by me or anyone else who's willing to contribute, to make the effect look even better.

summary: [GodotPhysics3D] RigidBody3D unnecessarily slides on surfaces where a cylindrical collision shape is used and angular axis lock is enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) Arc(tm) Graphics (MTL) - Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 7 155H (22 threads) - 15.08 GiB memory

### Issue description

When giving my RigidBody3d a cylindrical collission shape, it will slide on a StaticBody3D with an unrotated box collider, if All three* angular axis lock boxes are ticked.

Here is a demonstration:

![Image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microbullet/RigidBody3D-issue/refs/heads/main/axis%20lock.gif)

The same effect of "axis lock" can be achieved by enabling "Deactivation -> Freeze Rotation" which does not display this behaviour. However, this also disables the ability for this object to be moved by collision, which is not desirable for my project

* the force applied to the RigidBody3D has various levels of strength depending on which axis are "locked"


### Steps to reproduce

Simply create a RigidBody3D, giving it a ColissionShape3D that is a cylinder, and then select all three "Angular X/Y/Z" options before dropping it onto a StaticBody3D.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22580866/mrp.zip)

summary: Using an AtlasTexture as a frame for a SpritesFrames it causes visual oddities ONLY if the region of the texture is 1920x1080

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-9600K CPU @ 3.70GHz (6 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory ### Issue description Using an AtlasTexture as a frame for a SpritesFrames it causes visual oddities ONLY if the region of the texture is 1920x1080 If the height is 1920 you get the vertical lines and if your width is 1080 you get the horizontal lines ### Steps to reproduce - Download MRE - Open the SpritesFrames resource and observe the visual oddity - Go to the AtlasTexture resource and change the width and height of the region by at least one pixel - Go back to the SpritesFrames and observe that the oddities are gone ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mre.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22580599/mre.zip)

summary: X11: Fix minimization of maximized windows

description.text:
Currently, when a maximized window is minimized by...

- Clicking the minimize button on the title bar or the window button on task bar:
    `get_window().mode` still returns `WINDOW_MODE_MAXIMIZED`.
- Changing the window mode via code:
    Unminimizing the window by clicking the window button on task bar does not work for the first time, i.e., you have to click the button twice to see the window.

The first case is simple: "minimized" currently has less priority than other modes. Maximized and minimized are independent states for window managers and are not mutually exclusive. So reordering the checks solves the problem.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/b4472f4670a0b7fd34137d18f1e080e3319e3819/platform/linuxbsd/x11/display_server_x11.cpp#L3101-L3121

The second case is more or less messed up I think. I reverted some of the modifications in:

- #76868
    This one confuses window states stored in `WindowData` (minimized, maximized, fullscreen, etc.) with window modes. The former models states in window managers, while the latter is a Godot concept. It's a wrong fix for the issue it trys to solve, the problem still exists in another form.
- #65107
    This one is trying to fix the problem of initial window state not being honoured. But it's done by changing window states in `MapNotify`, i.e., whenever the window appears on the screen. This includes when the window is unminimized. This is the root cause of the problem.

The minimize / maximize states should use `_NET_WM_STATE`:

- Initial values are set using `XChangeProperty` before `XMapWindow`.
- Updates are received in `PropertyNotify`. We don't need to remember the window's state before minimization ourselves. After the user manually cancels minimization, the window manager will update the `_NET_WM_STATE` to the correct state, be it maximized or fullscreen.

I also removed the minimization state update logic in [`NoExpose`](https://tronche.com/gui/x/xlib/events/exposure/graphics-expose-and-no-expose.html) and [`VisibilityNotify`](https://tronche.com/gui/x/xlib/events/window-state-change/visibility.html). These two events are not related to window states at all. The former can only be received during `XCopyArea` or `XCopyPlane` calls which Godot does not use. The latter is about the window being obscured by other windows instead of the "visibility" in Godot term.

p.s. The fullscreen state may also have similar problems that have accumulated over the years. I took a quick look and it seems to be more complex, with mixed old and new code. Let's fix the problems one step at a time.
